title: Apprentice - Learning & Development
description:
NATS (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme) is our structured Apprenticeship Program aimed at providing hands-on learning opportunities only for fresh graduates / post graduates (Eligible Batch FY'21-FY'25) helping them build industry-ready skills & kick start their career with us.

Through this program, we’re investing in early talent by offering them mentorship & a strong foundation to grow with us .




Role Overview

We are looking for an enthusiastic and detail‐oriented Learning & Development (L&D)

Administrator to support the smooth running of L&D programs. This entry‐level role will focus

on managing data, training logistics, LMS administration, vendor coordination, and mandatory

training processes.




Key Responsibilities

Data Management: Collect data from stakeholders and prepare simple dashboards and review decks.
Training Logistics: Organize invites, logistics, meeting links, and feedback collection for in‐person and virtual programs.
L&D Communications: Share program updates and create basic visuals using Canva.
LMS Administration: Support backend tasks on the LMS, conduct monthly audits, and upload new courses.
Vendor & Finance Support: Assist with PR/PO requests, vendor onboarding, and payment tracking.
Mandatory Training: Share weekly reports and manage LMS updates for mandatory courses.




Skills & Qualifications

0-1 year experience, preferably in HR.
Basic knowledge of MS Excel and comfort with digital platforms.
Willingness to learn LMS tools and online learning platforms (Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, Udemy etc.).
Familiarity with Canva or interest in learning simple design tools.
Strong attention to detail and ability to stay organized.
Eagerness to learn and grow within the L&D function.
Proactively proposes actionable fixes rather than just reporting problems.

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Bengaluru

Please apply to Mumbai on our separate vacancy if that is closer to your location







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Bengaluru as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

title: Associate Engineer, NOC
description:
hackajob is collaborating with Scale Computing to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

Who We Are

Acumera is now Scale Computing! Scale Computing is a global leader in edge computing, hyperconverged infrastructure, and managed networking solutions. We deliver innovative, secure, andscalable technology that powers critical operations worldwide.

About The Role

Trainee/Associate Engineer will oversee daily operation of support desk functions that includes taking calls as first level support, entering the reported problems in the ticketing system, solving user problems, proper escalation of open problems and dealing with the customers. Will also participate in preventative maintenance, troubleshooting, configuring and providing technical

support for end-users.

Great Candidates Will Look Like This

A team player who can work well within the team and collaborate cross-functionally, especially in a remote environment.

An excellent communicator who is efficient and effective with customers and colleagues via instant messages, email, video conferencing and in-person interactions.

Enjoy troubleshooting and problem-solving analytically.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following: (Other duties may be assigned.)

Provide helpful and professional assistance to customers via phone, email and tickets.

Create or update support tickets as required for phone calls.

Document all calls, actions, and any relevant information in ticket notes.

Handle tickets created by customers via email.

Follow up on existing tickets proactively and within specified time frames.

Resolve or escalate tickets in a timely manner, within SLA guidelines.

Interact with other departments and teams, collaborating on tickets or solutions.

Evaluate customers’ needs and determine the appropriate action.

Troubleshoot complex technical issues with customers via phone.

Understand and follow security and other guidelines for customers’ accounts.

Process voicemails, creating or updating support tickets as necessary.

Write and send customized email responses to customers for specific situations.

Understand and adhere to Scale Computing policies and procedures.

Place outbound calls to resolve internally-detected issues.

Be professional and courteous at all times, especially when speaking or conversing with customers.

Technical Requirements

Knowledge of at least some of the following:

General computer systems

PCs, laptops, mobile devices.

Current and recent Operating Systems

Windows, Mac OS, Linux

Routing Protocols and concepts

Static and Dynamic routing, Distance, Priority, routing table, Default Gateway

Switching Protocols and concepts

VLAN, Trunks and link bonding, Spanning Tree and RSTP, MAC Address table,

ARP.

Networking Protocols and concepts

TCP, UDP, DHCP, DNS, VPN, ICMP, TELNET, SSH FTP, HTTP(s), IPSEC, SSL,etc.

Network troubleshooting tools such as PING, TRACEROUTE, NSLOOKUP, IPCONFIG, etc.


OSI Model – Advanced understanding of layers 1-4
Subnetting and Supernetting
Firewall rules and policies, Access Lists


Deep understanding of technical concepts within networking environments as well as security, risk and compliance.

Entry Level knowledge of Switches, Routers, Firewalls, and Networking in general is required. Must be detail-oriented and must be able to work well in a team environment.

Reporting Structure

Reports to Tier 1 Manager - NOC.

Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree (B.E./B.Tech/BCA) in a technical domain such as Computer Science, Telecommunication, Electronics & Communication, Information Technology, or a related field.

Minimum experience in the networking domain is preferred. (0-2 years)

Experience Working In Helpdesk Or Technical Support Is Preferred.

Industry certifications like CompTIA Network+, CCNA, CCNP, JNCIA, Wireshark, etc. are preferred.

Perks of Acumera

Group medical insurance.

Paid company holidays.

Casual dress code

Cab facilities for employees working in shifts.

Fully stocked snacks at office.

Vibrant and Inclusive Workplace Atmosphere.

Flexible work environment and an opportunity to grow as we grow.

Scale Computing is an equal-opportunity employer. All final candidates will be subject to a pre-employment background check.

title: HR Intern
description:
Instawork is on a mission to create meaningful economic opportunities for skilled hourly professionals in communities around the globe. Our AI-powered labor marketplace helps local businesses scale, and enables global technology companies to push the frontiers of robotics and AI. Backed by world-class investors like Benchmark, Spark Capital, Craft Ventures, Greylock, Y Combinator, and others, we’re looking for exceptional talent to reimagine the way the world works.

Location: Kormangala, Bangalore

Working Days: 6 Days a Week

Experience: Fresher (0–1 year)

Duration: 3 months

About the Role:

We are looking for a proactive and energetic HR Intern to support our day-to-day recruitment activities. This role is perfect for someone who wants to kick-start their career in HR, enjoys interacting with people, and thrives in a fast-paced startup environment.

Key Responsibilities:


Support end-to-end hiring for fresher and entry-level roles
Source, screen, and call candidates for various positions
Schedule and coordinate interviews with hiring panels
Maintain candidate trackers and ensure timely follow-ups
Assist in releasing offer letters and completing onboarding documentation
Ensure strong communication and coordination between candidates and internal stakeholders
Contribute to improving hiring processes and candidate experience



Requirements:


Good written and verbal communication skills
Strong organizational and follow-up abilities
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn
Comfortable working in a dynamic startup environment
Ability to manage multiple tasks at once
Available to work 6 days a week



Additional information:


Free Lunch + Snacks Daily
Cool workspace and fun team environment
Exclusive project goodies



Our Values


Empathy, Trust & Candor We put ourselves in the shoes of our colleagues and customers and don’t shy away from uncomfortable conversations, instead building trust through honest and direct feedback.
Bias for Action We practice high-velocity decision-making, clear-eyed that we often operate with incomplete information. Growing quickly means it’s OK to be wrong, so long as we learn from our mistakes and course correct!
Always Be Learning We’re a curious bunch, and with AI transforming our workplace we encourage everyone to learn from each other, compounding our knowledge and experience to help us change an entire industry.
Act Like an Owner We work long, hard, and smart, building products that delight our users and drive growth. Your ability to impact Instawork is limited only by your courage and conviction, not your job description.



About Instawork

Founded in 2015, Instawork is the nation’s leading online labor marketplace for food services, hospitality, light industry, and logistics, connecting more than 7M skilled workers with local restaurants, hotels, warehouses, stadiums, and more. Our AI-powered platform serves thousands of businesses across more than 50 major markets in the United States and Canada. We're not just helping fill shifts, we're supporting local economies—and we're just getting started!

Instawork has been featured by CBS News, The Wall Street Journal, The Washington Post, and the Associated Press. Forbes included us on their Next Billion Dollar Startups list; RetailTech Breakthrough named us Workforce Hiring Solution of the Year for 2025; and Inc. 5000 recognized us as one of the country's top 10% fastest-growing companies two years in a row. But what matters most is our impact. We're solving real problems for real people, and we’re doing it at scale.

Join our team to help us build something that matters! We’re looking for superstars who want to help us shape the future of work. With hubs in San Francisco, Bangalore, and Chicago, city offices in New York, Phoenix, and Singapore, we're back to working together in-person five days a week because we believe the best ideas happen when great people collaborate face-to-face. We also value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Learn more at www.instawork.com/about.

title: Program Associate (Talent and Partnerships)
description:
Avistrat works with aerospace engineers and organisations to build design capability and deploy engineering capacity. We engage with industry across two dimensions of scale:




Vertical scale — by deepening technical capability, and execution layers within specific aerospace domains.
Horizontal scale — by expanding across teams, programmes, and partner organisations where credible engineering capacity is required.




On the capability side, we support organisations by augmenting their design engineering functions and strengthening technical depth through structured, project-oriented engagement.

On the capacity side, we work closely with industry partners to identify, prepare, and deploy engineering talent aligned to real-world aerospace requirements.




Our work sits at the intersection of:




Engineering design
Industry readiness
Talent deployment




We could be called an EdTech, SkillsTech or an engineering services company but are building Avistrat as a scalable, integrated aerospace company — one that grows in depth, breadth, and responsibility while maintaining high standards of quality, credibility, and long-term partnership.

This is a high-rigour, outcome-driven environment where judgment, ownership, and execution matter more than volume or optics.




ROLE OVERVIEW




This is an entry-level, execution-focused role providing hands-on exposure to how talent pipelines and client delivery are executed in the aerospace domain.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




Candidate Intake & Decision Support:




- Own the first line of evaluation for candidates engaging with Avistrat

- Conduct structured conversations to assess readiness, seriousness, and fit

- Build judgment on who should move forward and who should not

- Prepare concise recommendations that directly inform leadership decisions




Talent Pipeline Ownership:




- Build and manage a live talent pipeline aligned to industry needs

- Shortlist candidates for internships, entry-level roles, and capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate execution from shortlisting to interview outcomes

- Track progress, identify bottlenecks, and close gaps proactively




Client & Partner Interface (Execution-Led):




- Support delivery of client capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate candidate–client interactions and timelines

- Gain exposure to real industry-facing execution with increasing responsibility




Process Building & Continuous Improvement:




- Convert live execution into repeatable processes and templates

- Analyse and improve tracking of candidates, conversations, and outcomes

- Contribute to making Avistrat’s talent pipeline scalable and predictable




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT




Not a sales role
Not a call-centre counselling role
Not an HR Admin role




WHAT WE LOOK FOR




A background and interest in the People or Human Resources function (academic or professional) is a must
Experience or demonstrated comfort with recruitment is a must
Some experience with aerospace would be ideal
Clear verbal and written communication
Comfort with ownership and follow-through
Ability to learn by doing and exploring fresh ideas
Ability to make and defend structured judgements
Attention to detail and professional conduct
Growth and improvement mindset
Drive to achieve goals




WHAT YOU WILL LEARN




How to thrive in a multi-stakeholder work environment
How to manage trade-offs for strategic decision-making
How quality of talent directly affects delivery outcomes
What enables successful client engagement
What the various elements of business operations are
How to grow and take ownership to drive work streams




GROWTH PATH




Freshers and early hires will join as interns for the first 3 months. Subsequently, the growth path could be expected as below:




0–6 months: Junior Talent and Partnerships Programme Associate (execution and judgment building)
6–12 months: Expanded ownership of intake, pipeline, and client delivery
12–18 months: Eligibility for progression to lead role, based on performance and business needs




There are various possible avenues along which career progression can be made based on candidate aspirations and business needs:




Ownership of the talent pipeline of Avistrat and organisational HR management
Programme management to manage a business function within Avistrat’s operating structure
Founder’s Office team to gain first-hand entrepreneurship insights




For further details about us, please visit our website: www.avistrat.com

title: Investment Banking Summer Analyst
description:
This role is for the Bengaluru office. Those across India can apply.







Our Client

Our subsidiary Global Markets Recruitment (“GMR”) is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on emerging and developed markets at entry to middle-management level.




We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.




Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.




You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst - Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications

Graduate – Analyst

At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets. Thereafter once selected, you will undergo our client one day assessment centre four interviews and one group role play. A decision will be made on the day.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

You will be well compensated for this time and the intern program will last for six weeks. We are running three programs from mid-June, end June and mid-July
Receive a bonus for your time
A permanent offer of employment and part of the Banks’ graduate program
Relocation is provided by the company. Starting salaries will be paid in USD for India $125,000 and up to $150,000 depending on experience
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers

title: Product Demonstration Specialist
description:
About Elevatoz Loyalty

Elevatoz Loyalty is a technology-led loyalty and engagement company that partners with leading businesses to drive sustainable sales growth through smarter channel engagement, rewards management, communication, and program execution. We design and operate high-impact loyalty ecosystems for channel partners, retailers, influencers, and other commercial stakeholders across industries. Our approach integrates technology, strategic consulting, servicing excellence, and operational rigor to deliver programs that are commercially effective, scalable, and deeply engaging for all participants.




Executive Summary

We seek an entry-level solution demo specialist (product demonstration specialist) to join Elevatoz Loyalty’s sales team in Bengaluru. This client-facing role suits a recent graduate (BCA or B.Sc. in Computer Science/IT) who is confident and tech-savvy. You will learn about our offerings in depth and deliver engaging software demonstrations, tailoring each demonstration to the prospect’s needs. Strong communication, enthusiasm towards talking to new people, and an eagerness to learn are essential for this position.




Role Overview

Conduct product and solution demonstrations for prospective clients and internal stakeholders.
Understand Elevatoz Loyalty’s platform features, modules, and use cases thoroughly.
Tailor demo narratives to different industries, client needs, and business contexts.
Collaborate with the sales and solution teams to align on demo objectives and outcomes.
Ensure every demo is polished, well-structured, and highlights real business value (not just features).
Continually improve demo scripts, environments, and presentation flow based on feedback.
Support internal teams with demo readiness, documentation, and assets.
Stay up-to-date with product updates and help standardize demo journeys for key solutions.




Key Responsibilities

Demo Delivery: Present clear, compelling live demos (in-person and virtual) of the Elevatoz Loyalty platform to prospects.
Customization: Adapt each demonstration to the client’s sector, size, and requirements, focusing on relevant use cases.
Collaboration: Work closely with sales, solution engineers, and product teams before demos to understand client goals.
Storytelling: Use strong storytelling and real-world examples to explain how our software solves client problems.
Value Emphasis: Go beyond features by emphasizing the practical business benefits of the solution.
Feedback Loop: Gather client feedback after demos and suggest enhancements to scripts and presentations.
Preparation: Assist in creating demo assets (slides, scripts, sandbox accounts) and ensuring the demo environment is ready.
Knowledge Building: Maintain deep knowledge of platform capabilities, industry trends, and competitor offerings.




Desired Candidate Profile

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Electronics, or a related field (e.g., BCA, B.Sc.). 
Experience: Fresh graduate or 0–1 year experience (internship experience in pre-sales, support, or tech roles is a plus).
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills; clear and polished presentation style.
Technical Understanding: Comfort with learning software platforms; ability to explain technology in simple, business-friendly language.
Confidence: Poised and self-assured in client-facing situations; able to handle questions and drive discussions.
Preparation & Storytelling: Strong preparation skills, logical organization, and an ability to craft a coherent demo narrative.
Attention to Detail: High level of professionalism and attention to detail in presentations and documentation.
Teamwork: Collaborative attitude and willingness to work with multiple stakeholders (sales, product, customer success).




Skills & Attributes

Presentation Skills: Proficiency with presentation tools (PowerPoint or similar) and demo software
Software Savvy: Basic familiarity with SaaS platforms or enterprise software environments.
Structured Thinking: Methodical approach to planning demos and addressing client needs step by step.
Client Orientation: Business-focused mindset; always relate features back to client outcomes.
Adaptability: Quick learner who can adjust to new product updates and client feedback.
Reliability: Consistency and polish in demo execution and follow-ups.




Why Join Us

High Impact Role: Be the face of our product in a high-visibility role that directly influences client decisions.
Learning & Growth: Rapidly develop skills in SaaS platforms, sales processes, and enterprise solutions by working closely with our sales and product experts.
Exposure: Engage with enterprise clients and strategic sales cycles, shaping how prospects perceive our platform and solutions.




Location & Employment Type

Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.
Employment Type: Full-time position.

title: Area Manager
description:
Description

Amazon is looking for bright leaders to lead large non-exempt teams across our fulfillment network! This is not a corporate, office-based position. This is a full-time position located within one of our Amazon fulfillment centers, sort centers, delivery stations or other operations buildings.

Amazon provides extensive training and development for entry level managers to become exceptional people leaders. Being an Area Manager means being on the front lines of our customer promise, ensuring your team delivers customers’ orders on time, by leading a large team of Amazon Associates in one of our fulfillment locations. You will have an opportunity to invest in others and develop a collaborative leadership style, while utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that have helped create the most customer-centric company on Earth.

What is fulfillment you ask? Fulfillment is how we refer to completing or fulfilling a customer’s Amazon.com order and the acts of picking, packing, shipping and delivering their order to meet or exceed their expectations! Our Operation’s workflow can be broken to three major lanes: first mile - where the product is housed and ready for your order; second mile - where your order is hauled to your area; and last mile - when the product is delivered to your door.

Please note that all lanes have slight building variations, but one thing is constant, our vision and dedication to the customer. As a reminder, our fulfillment centers are fast paced environments where standing, walking, and having on-the-production floor conversations are common as a visible supply chain leader!

As an Area Manager, you will have the opportunity to lead and develop your own team of Amazon Associates in a distribution warehouse environment. Your main focus will be motivating, mentoring, and managing your team to meet targeted goals. As a people manager, you will ensure that the team of Amazon associates has all the tools needed to succeed and maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, and attendance performance at work.

Key job responsibilities


Communicate policies and act as the primary information source for your team- maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed
Create, manage and support recognition programs
Support safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment
Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention
Partner with the management team to establish and maintain quality control standards
Develop performance goals and targets to achieve customer demand and ensure accuracy and quality


Must be able to perform the following tasks, with or without reasonable accommodation:


Stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours
Access all areas of building including ladders, stairs, gangways and shipping docks safely
Walk in/and around all areas of the warehouse with great frequency
Willing and able to work shift patterns that may include nights, weekends, and/or holidays based on business needs, regularly . Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend or be overnight.
Regularly bend, lift, stretch and reach both below the waist and above the head
Lift and move items up to 49 pounds


A day in the life

As an Operations Area Manager I, you lead teams by driving work from preparation through execution. Each shift, you set clear priorities, motivate associates and actively support operations while conducting safety observations and maintaining engagement. You balance operational demands with creating a safe, energized and inclusive environment. You manage daily task allocation, solve defined problems and communicate decisions to senior leaders. You deliver customer results by managing resources, auditing workflows and ensuring SLA adherence. Through coaching, 1:1s and performance evaluations, you develop talent, support career growth and build a cohesive, high-performing team.

About The Team

The Operations Team is dedicated to providing exceptional service to our customer, Amazon business units. As leaders, we pride ourselves on creating a team environment that lifts all members up. Our philosophy is simple: We all rise together. We combine diverse skills, experiences, and perspectives to deliver innovative solutions and continuously improve our service. Our strength lies in our unity, mutual support, and shared commitment to success. Together, we're building a culture of excellence that benefits our customers, our team, and the entire organization. In the Operations Team, every member plays a crucial role in our collective journey towards greatness.

Basic Qualifications


4+ years of professional or military experience
Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or experience in professional or military settings
Experience in project management
1+ years of data analysis in the facilities maintenance program, specifically in asset management and spare part optimization experience
Manage end-to-end IT asset lifecycle including procurement, tagging, deployment, tracking, maintenance, and disposal in compliance with company policies.
Coordinate device deployment and refresh activities, ensuring timely delivery, configuration, and handover to end users.
Oversee warehouse operations including receipt, storage, dispatch, and reconciliation of IT hardware and peripherals.
Ensure proper handling, storage, and security of IT assets to minimize loss, damage, or non-compliance.
Collaborate with IT, procurement, and vendors to support operational efficiency and business continuity.


Preferred Qualifications


1+ years of performance metrics, process improvement or lean techniques experience
1+ years of employee and performance management experience
2+ years of military experience


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. The Marketing Investment Optimization (MIO) vertical at Amgen is responsible for enabling data‑driven, decision‑grade marketing investment decisions across brands, channels, and portfolios.

Assist in facilitating Agile ceremonies including daily stand-ups, sprint planning, sprint reviews, and retrospectives.

Promote Agile principles and foster a culture of continuous improvement within IT teams.
Collaborate with developers, QA engineers, business analysts, and product owners to ensure alignment on sprint goals.

Excellent communications skills – written, verbal and visual.
High energy and solid work ethic, with motivational and leadership qualities.


Education And Experience


Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field.
Overall Work Experience: 5-7 years (at least 2 years’ experience in and around data analytics and engineering )

Location: Hyderabad, Telangana

Company: Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd.

About Us

Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd. (HUMS) is a dynamic and innovative company dedicated to transforming the healthcare industry. We take pride in being a reliable Healthcare Management partner. With several years of experience, we have perfected our end-to-end services in medical billing, A.R. management, and other essential healthcare operations.

We provide services to Harmony United Psychiatric Care, a US-based Healthcare Company. Our commitment to excellence, innovation, and continuous growth drives our success. As we expand, we are seeking enthusiastic and responsible freshers to join our team as Process Associates.

Position Overview

The Process Associate (Fresher) will support employee attendance tracking, workplace monitoring, and operational documentation. This is an entry-level role suitable for candidates who are willing to learn, follow company policies, and work closely with HR, IT, and Security teams.

Key Responsibilities

Attendance Monitoring


Assist in tracking daily employee attendance
Support verification of punctuality, absences, and late arrivals
Help generate daily, weekly, and monthly attendance reports
Share reports with HR and management teams


CCTV & Surveillance Support


Assist in monitoring CCTV systems to ensure workplace safety
Report suspicious activities or issues to the concerned team
Maintain basic logs of surveillance recordings


Login/Logout Monitoring


Support tracking of employee login and logout times
Maintain shift-wise working hour records
Identify basic discrepancies and report them to HR


Alarm & Access Control Support


Assist in maintaining alarm system codes and access permissions
Update access lists under supervision
Follow security procedures as per company policy


Operational Support


Assist HR and Operations teams with attendance-related tasks
Maintain records related to attendance and employee logs
Support onboarding activities such as system access setup
Assist during audits when required


Reporting & Documentation


Maintain organized records of attendance and login/logout data
Prepare reports under supervision
Provide required documents during internal reviews


Support & Coordination


Coordinate with HR, IT, and Security teams as needed
Provide support to employees regarding attendance queries
Follow instructions from supervisors and management


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in any discipline (Mandatory)
Freshers are welcome to apply
Basic computer knowledge (MS Excel, MS Word)
Good communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Willingness to learn and work in night shifts
High level of integrity and confidentiality


Diversity, Equality, and Inclusion

Diversity, equality, and inclusion are fundamental to our success at HUMS. We actively promote diversity across all aspects of our organization and foster an inclusive culture where everyone has equal opportunities to grow and succeed.

Work Schedule & Benefits


Competitive salary (including EPF and ESIC)
Health insurance

Opportunities for career growth and professional development
Food and cab-drop facilities available

title: Associate Data Scientist - Investment Analytics
description:
Level: Associate (L3)

Role: Marketing Investment Optimization – Investment Analytics

Mio Overview

The Marketing Investment Optimization (MIO) vertical at Amgen is responsible for enabling data‑driven, decision‑grade marketing investment decisions across brands, channels, and portfolios.

MIO brings together Investment Analytics (IA), Media Analytics (MA), and Marketing Science Operations (MSO) to measure effectiveness, quantify incrementality, and optimize marketing investments.

MIO focuses on marketing mix modelling (MMx), Experimentation and Causal Measurement, Optimization, Media and Campaign analytics, Scenario Planning, ensuring that marketing dollars are allocated to maximize business impact. The team partners closely with Brand Marketing, GCC/Agencies, CD&A, and MSO to translate complex analytics into actionable investment decisions.

Role Summary

The Associate – Investment Analytics role is an entry‑level analytics position within the Investment Analytics team. This role is ideal candidates who are excited about applying analytical thinking, structured problem solving, and data skills to real‑world business questions.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an Associate, you will work under guidance from Senior Associates and Managers to support analytical deliverables and build strong foundations in applied analytics.

You will:


Develop and apply advanced statistical models that help clients understand dynamic business issues.
Leverage analytic techniques to use data to guide decision-making.
Design custom analyses in R, Python and Excel to investigate and inform business needs.
Assist in preparing analysis summaries, documentation, and presentation materials.
Collaborate with team members to meet project timelines and quality standards and actively communicate project status updates.
Engage in ongoing learning to develop industry knowledge, and technical capabilities.


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's (3 to 6 Yrs) or master's (2 to 4 Yrs) degree required in any discipline with strong record of academic success in quantitative and analytic coursework such as operations research, applied mathematics, management science, data science, statistics, econometrics or engineering.
Experience in any analytical, data, or problem‑solving role.
Strong SQL skills and hands‑on experience with Python or R.


Preferred Qualifications


Demonstrated ability to perform structured QA and validation.
Experience working with time‑series and investment data.
Exposure to Marketing Mix Modelling (MMx) or econometric analysis.
Experience supporting analytics in pharma, healthcare, or regulated environments.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate closely with Senior Associates to deliver high‑quality analytical outputs.

title: Senior Analyst - Talent Immigration and Program Management - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

USI I-9 Program Senior Analyst

Work you’ll do

The USI I-9 Program Senior Analyst will execute the end-to-end I-9 verification process for U.S. workers to ensure internal and regulatory compliance.

Support end-to-end processes related to the physical and/or virtual I-9 employment verification of U.S. based workers (e.g. I-9 reverification, and I-9 completion for USI and Canada assignees), and other internal immigration programs that may arise, to ensure internal and regulatory compliance.

Initiate and manage a high volume of I-9 verifications, monitor I-9 completion for USI to US and Canada to US assignees, coordinate return from leave I-9 verifications and process.

Respond to questions and provide information to workers and Workplace Experience regarding the I 9 process, acceptable documentation, agent completion, physical inspection, return from leave, etc.


Correcting I-9 forms when necessary/possible.
Escalate red flag issues to the I-9 Program Lead and/or Internal Programs Manager

The Immigration Programs & Strategy team is part of the Talent Immigration organization of Deloitte Services LP. This team is responsible for supporting the needs of our foreign national practitioners across all businesses. Do you want to help solve problems that drive success in the rapidly evolving technology and connectivity landscape? title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description:
As part of the Commercial Engineering organization, you will be part of a fast-paced, entrepreneurial, energetic, and high performing team at the heart of the Thomson Reuters transformation to developing modernized applications and services. This group is nimble, creative and shapes our technology roadmap.  This role will be instrumental to enabling the current and future world of the digital experience at Thomson Reuters. You will be challenged with identifying innovative ideas and proof of concepts to deliver against existing and future needs of our customers. The role will report to the Director of Quality Engineering within Commercial Platform and work across the application and platform engineering teams to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for our customers.

Quality Assurance Engineers are entry/mid-level professionals that monitor and fine tune automation framework capabilities while driving innovative automated testing solutions focused on the customer in a team-oriented environment. A QA Engineer may assist their colleagues and more junior QA Engineer team members by solving problems, providing technical guidance, training and mentoring others. Exceptional attention to detail, strong analytical skills, excellent communication skills and deep technical ability in performance testing, automation testing methodologies, tools and processes within an agile environment will be key for success.

About The Role

In this opportunity as Quality Assurance Engineer, you will:


Design and implement – automated testing solutions by understanding business needs, defining test scenarios, building test scripts using tools/frameworks and comprehensive understanding of software development life cycle/Agile methodologies. Test applications across a range of technologies including web, mobile, batch processing, CMS, REST services.
Be a Team Player – work in a collaborative team-oriented environment, share information, value diverse ideas, partner with cross functional and remote teams. Coach and assist less experienced quality assurance engineers.
Be an Agile Person – involved in the sprint team. Communicate on issues that may affect QA sprints, identify process improvements, & work with technical team to ensure sprint success. Review user stories providing feedback on testability and effort estimations from quality assurance perspective.


About You

You're a fit for the role of Quality Assurance Engineer if your background includes:


4+ years Hands-on experience on SAP Functional testing
S4 hana functional testing, test automation tool - work soft
Experience on automation test tool (work soft)
Automation Testing (TOSCA/Work soft)
Experience on JIRA/ ADO.
Experience in Agile methodology.
Hands-on experience with white box and black box testing.


What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

description:

Job Type: Full time

Location: Hyderabad, India (Hybrid)

Status: Accepting Candidates

Number of positions:1

Description:

A Global Infrastructure Organization, including Information Technology, provides IT solutions and support services with a flexible, scalable model. As a member of this high performance, progressive team, you will play a key role as a member of staff managing IT Scrum team to help meet its growth and profitability goals.




Primary Accountabilities:

IT Scrum Master: Leads IT development scrum teams in providing IT solutions to complex, high-impact business problems from requirements through implementation of integrated solutions and/or infrastructure services. Has ongoing accountability for all processes, resources, and deliverables related to online development. Oversee the team of internal and external IT resources as they plan, track and execute development from start to completion.

• Define and manage scope, goals, and deliverables aligned with business objectives.

• Develop and maintain plans, schedules, milestones, and budgets.

• Facilitate Agile ceremonies including daily stand-ups, sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives.

• Coordinate cross-functional teams and stakeholders to ensure alignment and collaboration.

• Coach and guide Agile teams on Scrum/Agile principles, frameworks, and practices.

• Support the Product Owner with backlog refinement, prioritization, and user story clarity.

• Identify, log, and manage risks and issues throughout the project lifecycle.

• Remove impediments that block team progress and delivery.

• Monitor team velocity, burn-down charts, and other Agile metrics to track performance and identify improvement areas.

• Communicate project and sprint status to leadership and stakeholders clearly and regularly.

• Promote a culture of continuous improvement, psychological safety, and high performance within the team.

• Ensure that deliverables meet quality standards, scope, and timeline expectations.

• Manage stakeholder expectations, communication plans, and engagement strategies.

• Lead governance activities, including status reports, steering committee meetings, and project reviews.

• Ensure compliance with organizational processes, procurement policies, and vendor management (if applicable).

• Adapt processes and practices based on team maturity, organizational needs, and development complexity.




Skills & Qualifications:

• Strong understanding of Agile frameworks (Scrum, SAFe, or Kanban) and traditional project management (Waterfall, Hybrid)

• Understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)

• Experience with project planning, scheduling, and budget management

• Familiarity with Agile metrics (velocity, burn-down charts, cumulative flow diagrams)

• Sprint planning, backlog grooming, and release management skills

• Experience managing scrum teams across multiple Web/ERP/CRM/CIAM/Middleware platforms preferred

• Extensive experience working as part of a global, virtual team in a multinational company

• Excellent facilitation skills for stand-ups, retrospectives, and stakeholder meetings

• Conflict resolution and team coaching skills

• Strong verbal and written communication across technical and non-technical audiences

• Skilled in status reporting, dashboard creation, and executive updates

• Proficient in identifying and managing project risks, issues, and scope changes

• Proficiency in tools like JIRA, Confluence, Azure DevOps, Trello, or equivalent Agile tools

• Experience with MS Project, Smartsheet, or other tools

• Education/Experience: Bachelors or University Degree or equivalent.Typically 8+ years relevant experience for entry to this level. Requires specialized depth and/or breadth of expertise in job.

title: GTM Associate
description:
An accelerated Leadership Track for fresh graduates and recent graduates (0-2 years) who want to build, learn, and lead early: compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.




The Opportunity

Designed for an extremely limited set of exceptional fresh graduates stepping into their early full-time role, this position offers a non-linear, accelerated path to leadership. The Founder’s Office – GTM Associate is a high-intensity track built around real ownership, steep learning curves, and close collaboration with founders and senior leaders — compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.

You’ll work on high-impact initiatives across growth, strategy, customer engagement, and market expansion. Top performers quickly take on ownership, lead teams, and drive initiatives across geographies. This is not a conventional entry-level job. This is a launchpad for future business leaders.




What you'll do

Work directly with founders and senior leaders on GTM strategy, execution, and experimentation
Drive market expansion and international go-to-market initiatives
Own cross-functional projects across sales, marketing, customer success, partnerships, and product
Lead revenue growth and funnel optimization initiatives
Analyze markets, customers, and performance data to identify growth opportunities
Engage with global customers to uncover pain points and build customer-first solutions
Support and lead new launches, pilots, and strategic projects
Collaborate across geographies and functions to deliver outcomes
Take end-to-end ownership of initiatives and be accountable for results
Over time, you will move from: Execution → Ownership → Market/Function Leadership → Team Leadership / P&L Responsibility
Our expectation is clear: 




Within ~2 years, high performers will be operating at a team-lead level. This program is built for people who want accelerated responsibility, not gradual hand-holding.




Who we're looking for

We seek outliers and you might be a fit if you have:

Undergraduate degree in any discipline from a Tier 1 institution, completed by June 2026 (or final examinations completed by then). Open to recent graduates with 0–2 years of experience
Willingness to commit to a minimum of two years with the organization
Strong spoken and written English communication
High learning agility, curiosity, and initiative-taking ability
Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven mindset—you see problems as opportunities
Strong business aptitude, logical reasoning, and comfort with numbers and data
Structured, strategic thinking with exceptional attention to detail
Proven problem-solving skills and customer empathy
Passionate hustler mentality with the drive to execute, not just plan
Excited by ambiguity, high expectations, and real responsibility
Aspiration to become a future business leader, not just a functional specialist
Hunger to work on cutting-edge, industry-leading problems
Bonus: Proficiency in a foreign language




About DeltaX

Founded in 2012, DeltaX is an AI & Automation company driving transformation primarily in the area of Marketing & Advertising. Our flagship product is a data-driven digital advertising platform designed for agencies and advertisers to seamlessly plan, create, buy, track, attribute, and report campaigns across search, social, display RTB, mobile, video, and other digital channels. Its cutting-edge technology empowers marketing teams worldwide with deep insights, automated ROI-based spend optimisation, and the ability to execute award-winning, technology-driven campaigns. The scope of our offering has compounded in width and depth with AI and automation being at the forefront of it.

title: Associate Benefits Support Services Analyst
description:
Job Description

TriNet is a leading provider of comprehensive human resources solutions for small to midsize businesses (SMBs). We enhance business productivity by enabling our clients to outsource their HR function to one strategic partner and allowing them to focus on operating and growing their core businesses. Our full-service HR solutions include features such as payroll processing, human capital consulting, employment law compliance and employee benefits, including health insurance, retirement plans and workers’ compensation insurance.

TriNet has a nationwide presence and an experienced executive team. Our stock is publicly traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol TNET. If you’re passionate about innovation and making an impact on the large SMB market, come join us as we power our clients’ business success with extraordinary HR.

Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that many potential applicants discourage themselves from applying to jobs unless they meet every single requirement. TriNet always strives to hire the most qualified candidate for a particular role, ensuring we deliver outstanding results for our small and medium-size customers. So, if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every single qualification in the job description, nobody’s perfect – and we encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles.

Job Summary

As an Associate Benefits Support Services Analyst, you will support the Benefits Core Services team in delivering high-quality assistance for TriNet’s employee benefits programs. This entry-level role is ideal for individuals with foundational knowledge in HR or benefits administration who are eager to grow in a global, fast-paced environment. You will assist with benefits inquiries, perform research, and support the processing of benefits events, ensuring a smooth experience for clients and worksite employees (WSEs).

This role is responsible for managing a broad range of HR and benefits-related processes, including employee data updates, benefits reporting, and support for service orders and rehires. Key responsibilities span COBRA administration and compliance, FSA/HSA claims processing and adjudication, 401(k) plan support, and end-to-end Leave of Absence (LOA) management. The position involves handling escalations, coordinating with third-party vendors, ensuring data accuracy across systems, and maintaining compliance with regulatory standards. Strong attention to detail, effective communication, and the ability to manage multiple workflows are essential for success in this role.

Key Responsibilities


Respond to general benefits inquiries from clients and WSEs via phone, chat, email, or case management systems
Assist in researching and resolving routine to moderately complex benefits-related tasks
Support the processing of PeopleSoft benefits events under supervision
Participate in outbound communication for follow-ups or benefits renewal coordination
Assist in auditing and reviewing benefits data for accuracy
Collaborate with team members and participate in training and knowledge-sharing sessions



Qualifications

Education


Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (preferred)
Fresh graduates with relevant internships or coursework are encouraged to apply
Experience
0–2 years of experience in HR operations or benefits administration
Exposure to HRIS platforms like PeopleSoft is a plus



Skills & Competencies


Basic proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook)
Strong communication skills (written and verbal)
Willingness to learn and adapt to new systems and processes
Analytical thinking and attention to detail
Good organizational and time management skills
Customer service orientation and active listening
Ability to work independently and in a team environment



Work Environment


Work in a clean, pleasant, and comfortable office work setting. The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable persons with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
This position is 100% in office.



Please Note: TriNet reserves the right to change or modify job duties and assignments at any time. The above job description is not all encompassing. Position functions and qualifications may vary depending on business necessity.

TriNet is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate against applicants based on race, religion, color, disability, medical condition, legally protected genetic information, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, gender identity or expression, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth or related medical conditions), age, veteran status or other legally protected characteristics. Any applicant with a mental or physical disability who requires an accommodation during the application process should contact recruiting@trinet.com to request such an accommodation.

title: Seller Operations Executive
description:
About noon

We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to deliver to every door every day. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Seller Operator (Operations) who can help us move even faster.




Our mission: Ring every doorbell, every day.




What you'll do:

At noon, you’ll work alongside some of the fastest, smartest, and most driven people in the region. Our teams are young, ambitious, and relentlessly execution-focused, working on high-impact initiatives every day.




Overview of the role:

Looking for a dynamic Operations Associate to support forward logistics, reverse operations, and seller onboarding. The role involves managing order flows, handling returns, and assisting sellers to ensure smooth marketplace operations. Ideal for candidates who are detail-oriented, eager to learn, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.




What you'll need:

1. Forward Operations:

Assist in the day-to-day execution of forward logistics operations.
Support order processing activities to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
Collaborate with team members to maintain optimal inventory levels and smooth shipment processes.
Learn and contribute to the optimization of supply chain operations.




2. Reverse Operations:

Support the management of the reverse logistics process, including returns and exchanges.
Assist in the implementation of effective return policies to minimize costs and enhance customer satisfaction.
Analyze return trends under guidance to provide insights for process improvement.
Work closely with the team to address and resolve return-related inquiries.




3. Seller Onboarding:

Contribute to the onboarding process for new sellers onto our platform.
Assist in maintaining up-to-date onboarding documentation for sellers.
Provide support in training sessions for sellers and act as a point of contact for basic inquiries.
Collaborate with team members to streamline and enhance the overall onboarding process.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or equivalent practical experience.
Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Entry-level candidates or 1-3 years of relevant work experience. -Strong organizational skills with attention to detail. -Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment. -Eagerness to learn and contribute to process improvement.




Who will excel?

We’re looking for candidates who thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic start-up environment.
We’re searching for problem solvers, people who operate with a bias for action and have a deep understanding of the importance of resourcefulness over reliance.
Candor is our only default. Demanding unequivocal high standards should be non-negotiable because quality matters.
We want people who are radically candid, cohorts who commit to settling for nothing but the best - in hiring, in accepting work from colleagues, and in your own work. Ours is not an easy mission, but it is a meaningful one.
Every hire must actively raise the bar of talent in the company to help us reach our vision.

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Bengaluru

Please apply to Mumbai on our separate vacancy if that is closer to your location







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Bengaluru as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

title: Securities & Derivatives Analyst
description:
The Securities & Derivatives Analyst is an entry level position responsible for processing orders and transactions originating from trading desks and branch offices in coordination with the Operations - Transaction Services team. The overall objective of this role is to assist in the clearance, settlement and investigation of client securities and derivatives transactions.

Responsibilities:


Identify and implement process improves of the securities and derivatives settlement process using data analysis tools and methodologies
Complete operational tasks focused on administering defined procedures, analyses and report preparation
Analyze data and make recommendations for the launch of new products and services or upgraded platforms
Minimize risk to the bank by monitoring errors and suggesting solutions that reduce errors, and ensure adherence to audit and control policies
Identify policy gaps and propose efforts to streamline the securities and derivatives settlement processes
Monitor, analyze and evaluate processes and data to ensure the accuracy and quality of completed settlement tasks
Maintain an in-depth understanding of how the team collaborates with other areas in accomplishing assigned tasks
Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.


Qualifications:


2-4 years of relevant experience
Fundamental knowledge of securities/derivatives activities, policies, procedures, and concepts
Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills
Primary role is in EMEA work time , but candidate must be flexible to work in APAC and NAM time zones as well.


Education:


High School diploma or equivalent

title: Campus Recruiter
description:
Campus Recruiter will be responsible for building strong relationships with universities and colleges, managing campus hiring for various programs, and attracting top entry-level talent to meet organizational needs for Pune region. This role involves planning, executing, and optimizing campus recruitment strategies to ensure a strong talent pipeline.




Areas of Responsibility:

Campus Engagement:

Develop and maintain relationships with training and placement team, career development centre of institute, HOD and student organizations.
Design and execution of Pre & Post Offer Student Engagement strategy.
Strategize and contribute to effective branding initiatives at institutes within the region.
Representing the company at campus events, career fairs, and networking sessions.
Promote "TCS Campus Commune" as the leading social media learning platform.

Recruitment Process:

Understanding the hiring requirements from different units and coordinating with the CTAG to get the requirements fulfilled.
Manage end-to-end campus hiring, including branding and promotion at various channels of social media, planning the drive, interview closure, document validation and offer release.
Coordinate assessment centers, technical evaluations, publishing the dashboards with internal and external stakeholders.
Engaging BU leadership for driving niche skill hiring in the region
Organizing and Promoting participation for TCS Flagship contests like EngiNX, Codevita, EnQuode, HackQuest, Inframind, HumAIn etc.
Connect with senior leadership in the branch to ensure an effective pipeline of speakers at various events.
Staying updated about hiring trends and best practices and ensuring the process is being automated and made efficient with periodical intervals. Partnering with the team to design, refine, and implement innovative recruitment strategies and processes.
Work closely with managers to gain a comprehensive understanding of the company’s hiring needs for every role, and to meet competitive hiring goals and expectations.
Ensuring TAT and SLA are followed while submitting the reports, dashboards and while resolving queries for candidates or placement officers.
